instruction: Classify the churn reason expressed in the customer's cancellation message.
baseline_prompt: You are a customer-retention analyst. Classify the churn reason expressed in the customer's cancellation message. Classify into one of these categories: price, coverage, service, speed, moving, competitor.
source: Authored for TEI-Bench (Sonnet-generated, Sonnet-validated; kept only label-agreed examples).

train.query: My family is trying to save money and we went through all our monthly bills. Unfortunately, yours is one of the highest and we found a similar option that costs significantly less. We have to go with the more affordable choice.
The signal at my house is practically nonexistent. I have to walk outside just to send a text message. This is unacceptable and I'm switching to another provider.

I've had the worst experience trying to resolve a simple issue with my equipment. Each time I contact support, I get conflicting information from different agents. One person tells me to do one thing, the next person says something completely different. The lack of consistency and knowledge among your staff is absolutely unacceptable.

After multiple complaints about how long it takes to do anything online with your service, I've decided to switch providers. Simple tasks that should take seconds end up taking minutes. The throughput is nowhere near what was promised, and I've had enough of waiting around.
My lease is ending and I'm relocating to another city for graduate school. Checked and you don't have availability in that location, so please process my cancellation.

After comparing plans, I'm moving my service to T-Mobile. They have a promotional rate that includes streaming services bundled in, and frankly it's hard to pass up given what they're offering new customers right now.

The promotional rate I had was great, but now that it expired the regular rate is just too steep for me. I'm a student and can't afford what you're asking for the standard plan. I need something more budget-friendly.
The reception in my apartment is terrible. Calls break up constantly and I can't even load basic websites. My neighbor has a different carrier with perfect signal, so I'm making the switch.

Your representatives were incredibly rude when I called about billing errors on my account. The agent I spoke with yesterday talked over me and refused to listen to my concerns. When I asked to speak to a supervisor, I was told none were available and then the call mysteriously dropped. This is no way to treat paying customers.

The main reason I'm leaving is that your network performance has deteriorated significantly. Downloads that used to complete in minutes now take hours, and streaming anything above standard definition is just not feasible. I need a provider that can deliver the throughput necessary for modern internet usage.
We've sold our house and are relocating to a rural area where your infrastructure doesn't reach. Will need to cancel our plan effective at the end of this month.
I'm porting my number to Google Fi. They have this international travel benefit that works way better for my business trips, and their customer acquisition deal right now is too good to ignore.

Honestly, I love the product, but the cost has become prohibitive. I did the math and found another company that can give me what I need for almost 40% less than what I'm currently paying you. It's a financial decision I have to make.
My phone shows no service in my office building, which is in the downtown area where I'd expect full bars. I've complained multiple times but nothing has improved, so I'm done.

I've been trying to get help with my account for three weeks now and nobody ever calls me back. Every time I reach out, I get transferred to different departments and have to explain my problem all over again. I'm tired of being put on hold for an hour just to get disconnected. This is ridiculous and I'm done.

test.query: I'm canceling because the latency issues are making it impossible to enjoy any online activities. Gaming is completely out of the question with this much delay, and even loading social media feeds feels painfully slow. I've tested my connection multiple times and the results are consistently far below acceptable levels.
I've accepted a job offer in another state and will be relocating next month, so I need to cancel my account since you don't provide service in that area.

I've decided to switch to Verizon because they're offering me unlimited data plus a free phone upgrade, which is a much better deal than what I'm currently getting with you. My last day will be the 15th of next month.

After looking at my expenses, I realized I'm spending way more than I should on this subscription. A friend showed me what they're paying with another provider and it's literally half of what you're charging me for basically the same thing. I have to make the switch.

There's literally no signal at my new workplace and the surrounding area has very spotty reception. Since I need to be reachable during business hours, I have to find a carrier that actually works where I spend most of my time.

I spent two hours on chat yesterday trying to get a simple question answered and the agent kept giving me generic copy-paste responses that didn't address my actual problem. When I asked for clarification, they just repeated the same unhelpful information. It's clear nobody actually reads what customers are saying.

The connection has become unbearably sluggish over the past few months. Pages take ages to load, and I frequently get timeout errors. I've tried everything from resetting my router to optimizing my device settings, but nothing improves the situation. I can't run my online business with these performance issues.
I'm a college student graduating and returning to my home country permanently, so I won't need this service anymore once I leave next week.

My family and I are switching over to Mint Mobile. They contacted us with a plan that's significantly cheaper per month and honestly the features they're advertising seem comparable to what we have now.

I've been with you for three years, but honestly I just can't justify the monthly bill anymore. My budget is tight right now and I need to cut expenses wherever I can. I compared what I'm paying to what's actually in my bank account each month and something has to give.
I've been having constant dropped calls in my neighborhood and the bars on my phone rarely go above two. I can't rely on this connection anymore, so I need to cancel my account.

Every interaction with your company has been frustrating and exhausting. Wait times are endless, representatives lack basic product knowledge, and follow-up promises are never kept. I've documented six separate occasions where someone said they'd call me back within 24 hours and never did. I can't continue dealing with this level of incompetence.

Unfortunately, I need to discontinue my service because the bandwidth has been consistently underwhelming. I'm paying for high-tier internet, yet I can barely manage to upload files or participate in remote meetings without constant lag. My colleagues on other networks have no such problems, which makes this especially frustrating.
Got transferred by my employer to the west coast office, so I'll be leaving town in two weeks. Since you're not available there, I need to end my subscription.

I've accepted an offer from Cricket Wireless that includes three months at half price plus no activation fees. Given that several of my coworkers switched recently and recommended it, I've decided to make the move as well.

The value just isn't there anymore for what I'm being charged. When I first signed up the rate was reasonable, but after all these increases over the past year it's gotten out of hand. I can't keep paying this much.
I live in a rural area and was told I'd have adequate connectivity, but I barely get one bar and data doesn't work at all. I need something more reliable for my home business.

The technician who came to my house last Tuesday was unprofessional and left without fixing anything. He barely looked at the problem, said it wasn't his job, and rushed out. When I called to complain, the supervisor was defensive and dismissive instead of apologizing or offering a solution.
